translated: I hope you'll check out the side stories as well.
Everyone is saying how cute they are.
Seriously.
They're so good.
I think it's really rare to see Niigo wearing white.
Having done so many AfterTalks up until now...
Mafuyu...
You know,
Mafuyu isn't quite smiling, but seeing her with more of a smile component made me so happy. It was an event story that really cleared my heart, so...
I thought it was wonderful how all the illustrations had such a sense of warmth to them.
Thank you very much.
So, that was the illustration corner. Moving on,
for this event, Onuma Parsley-san wrote a new song for us.
We're going to watch the 3DMV for 'Kazarite' and talk about the song.
Before we watch the 3DMV, we received a comment from the SEGA 3DMV production team, so let me introduce it.
In the 3DMV for 'Kazarite,' the theme is the emotions Mizuki can't honestly express to the other members, shown through a sequence of falling into water.
We used the insert function this time, and for the underwater falling sequence, we spent a lot of time...
...expressing it through a lot of trial and error.
There are still challenges in expressing deep emotions,
but we hope you can see this new form of expression through Mizuki, they said.
Well then, let's take a look at the 3DMV.
Did you see that expression just now?
They're smiling.
It's so good.
I was honestly blown away when I first saw this 3DMV.
Like,
I was like, 'Wow, the scenery changes this much?'
There's a school-like setting that looks like after school, and a shot through a window with leaves reflected in it.
And when they're walking down the hallway, you can see things in the light...
Not Wi-Fi... what do you call it? It's like dust or particles floating in the air.
The direction is so detailed.
I wondered how they even did that.
It was amazing.
The way everyone rotates and the person changes,
the production side of it was so incredible it surprised me.
It was really great.
It's out now so you can look back at it.
The 2DMV is also really good, so please watch that too.
Regarding the 2DMV, this is just what I thought,
it hasn't been explicitly stated, so I'm not sure, and I haven't heard anything from Mizuki yet, so I still don't know...
In the 2DMV, there's a spot that looks like a birthday party, like a seat of honor.
Mizuki is at a place that looks like a cake, and the camera pans over things that symbolize Mizuki.
Things are shown, like the letter from their older sister,
and ribbons... those things appearing was really nice. The 2D version.
Both were amazing.
I was surprised by how much information was packed in.
Also, regarding the song... I felt that Mizuki's songs usually had a lot of painful words in the lyrics.
I'm not comparing it to past songs, but I really felt that this song has a lot of positive lyrics.
But even within that, there are those swaying emotions inside Mizuki.
I don't think they're the type of person who can let everything out.
I consulted with the director about the nuances and how far to go,
talking about how much strength to add or subtract while creating the song.
There are parts where the sound becomes like a radio,
and I asked, 'Can I try making my voice sound a bit raspy here to really bring out the emotion?'
We consulted on various nuances like that for this song.
I hope you'll enjoy both the full 2DMV and the 3DMV where Mizuki smiles at you.
Both are already uploaded to the official ProSeka YouTube channel,
so I hope you'll check them out.
And with that, our time is almost up. This concludes the ProSeka AfterTalk for the 'Reaching Out for an Unbreakable Tomorrow' event.
To everyone watching the stream, how was it?
Let's take a look at the comments.
Someone said, 'Onuma Parsley-san's MV and the Niigo 2D version have different vibes, and both are great.'
That's right.
Mizuki is... how should I put it?
Students thinking about their future... and ProSeka is also approaching its 5th anniversary.
Mizuki has been through a lot leading up to the 5th anniversary. In the midst of that, they look forward.
Things happened that made them look forward, but I...
It depicts that feeling of 'I'm going to do my best,' or 'Boku will do my best.'
I think the Niigo version of 'Kazarite' feels like that, but Onuma-san's version...
How should I say it?
It's like an MV about a tired working adult who's still trying their best to look forward and encourage themselves.
I felt that even with the same lyrics, depending on the singer and the person singing,
the song itself can have a very wide range of expression.
I thought that about 'Kazarite.' Please check out Onuma-san's version too.
What do you call it? The 2D version?
